texto: The Christian is free from all other human beings. He does not have to live over against others, controlled by their actions and responses. Rather, he lives according to Christ's commands. This is Christian freedom. It is a freedom unknown by others. It is not just when others do the things that we like that we act properly toward them; we are free to do good even when they don't because our actions are not dependent on their responses. It is the Lord Christ when we serve!
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jay E. Adams: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The Christian is free from all other human beings. He does not have to live over against others, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, christ, christianity pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e christianity.

texto: Evil is powerful, but good is more powerful. In fact, evil is so powerful that only good has the power to overcome evil. Darkness can be driven away only by light.
explicacao: Citação de Jay E. Adams sobre temas ligados a superacao. “Evil is powerful, but good is more powerful. texto: When is a train most free? Is it when it goes bouncing across the field off the track? No. It is free only when it is confined (if you will) to the track. Then it runs smoothly and efficiently, because that was the way that its maker intended for it to run. It needs to be on the track, structured by the track, to run properly. You too need to be on the track. God's track is found in God's Word.

No. It is …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, counseling, spiritual-growth, com foco em dia-chuvoso e termino.
